description: Modified _ingest_dir to skip .git and other hidden directories by default.
title: Skip hidden directories during directory ingestion

description: Refine _discover to avoid broad Exception catches and provide more specific feedback.
title: Improve ingestor discovery error handling

description: Implement mode='api' for backends. The [api] section in the TOML specifies: url, method, headers. The [api.body] section provides a template string supporting $prompt_json (prompt JSON-encoded as a string with quotes/newlines escaped), $model, and $VARNAME (env var) substitution. The [api.response] section provides an extract jq-style expression (e.g. .content[0].text) to pull text from the HTTP response. Also add a clear error when a CLI backend command is not found in PATH: 'Backend <id> requires <command> which was not found in PATH'.
title: backends: HTTP API mode

description: Currently extract_json finds the first and last brackets, which might include non-JSON text between multiple arrays/objects.
title: extract_json incorrectly combines multiple JSON blocks

description: Ingest currently reads whole files into memory; should handle large logs more gracefully.
title: Add resource limits/streaming to ingest for large files

description: Add support for IPv6 and refine the IPv4 regex for better accuracy.
title: Improve ip_address redaction

close_reason: Session, Exchange, ToolCall, Outcome dataclasses with to_dict/from_dict. 12 tests, 98% coverage.
description: Define the core data model in khipu/model.py — Session, Exchange, ToolCall, Outcome dataclasses with to_dict/from_dict serialization. This is the normalized format all ingestors produce and all analyzers consume.
title: Trace model: Session, Exchange, ToolCall, Outcome dataclasses

description: Use tiktoken or a better heuristic than len/4 for Claude-targeted token estimation.
title: Use more accurate token estimation

description: Expose three top-level functions from the khipu package so users can do: from khipu import ingest, analyze, emit. Currently __init__.py only sets __version__. Also per spec, analyze should be async (async def analyze(...)) with a synchronous convenience wrapper analyze_sync for contexts without asyncio. The current synchronous analyze() in analyze.py can become analyze_sync; the async version wraps it or implements true async if backends support it.
title: library: public API exports in __init__.py

description: Implement config.toml support. Format: TOML with sections [llm] (backend, model), [security] (redact bool, custom_patterns list, passthrough_backends list), [analysis], [condensation], [output]. Discovery order (highest priority first): .khipu/config.toml (project-local), ~/.config/khipu/config.toml (user-global), built-in defaults. Settings are merged at key level. Built-in defaults: backend='claude-api', model='claude-sonnet-4-6', redact=true. Wire config into the pipeline so the CLI and analyze() respect configured backend/model defaults. Custom redaction patterns from [security].custom_patterns are applied in addition to default patterns.
title: config: config.toml format, discovery, and pipeline wiring

description: Add docstrings/comments to explain the topological sort implementation in analyze.py.
title: Document topo_sort DFS logic more clearly

description: Add --transport sse option to the 'khipu mcp' command. When started with SSE transport, the server accepts SSE connections over a network port instead of communicating over stdin/stdout. Depends on the base MCP server (stdio) being implemented first.
title: mcp-server: SSE transport mode

close_reason: emit.py implemented with json and markdown (Jinja2) emitters; --emit flag added to CLI; 15 tests passing
description:
Implement the emit stage. emit(result, template='markdown') renders an AnalysisResult through a Jinja2 template. Default markdown template produces a human-readable report with sections for workflows, patterns, and crystallization scores.

Also implement 'json' emitter: emit(result, template='json') returns json.dumps of the AnalysisResult as a dict. No Jinja2 needed for json — just serialize directly.

--emit flag on the CLI accepts 'markdown' (default) or 'json'. The json emitter enables piping: khipu analyze ./traces/ --emit json | jq '.patterns[]'.
title: Emitter: Jinja2 markdown output template

description:
Implement deterministic condensation (no LLM). Strip tool call I/O bodies, keep tool names/file paths/success booleans. First 100 chars of human messages, 80 chars of agent messages. Output structured JSON summary per session.

Auto mode: estimate tokens as len(json_text)/4, condense if sessions exceed 70% of backend context_limit. Flags: --condense (always), --no-condense (never), --max-sessions N (explicit cap).

Overflow handling: when sessions exceed context_limit even after condensation, take the most recent sessions that fit and emit a stderr warning stating how many were skipped. --no-condense must error (not warn) if sessions exceed limit.
title: Condensation: deterministic session compression

description: Add a third built-in emitter 'claude_md' as a .j2 template. It produces a guidance block for appending to CLAUDE.md, formatted with ALWAYS:, NEVER:, WATCH OUT:, and AUTOMATE: prefixes derived from crystallization candidates. The block is wrapped in <\!-- KHIPU:BEGIN --> / <\!-- KHIPU:END --> markers. Depends on the .j2 discovery system being in place.
title: emit: add claude_md built-in emitter

description: Add 'khipu mcp' CLI command that starts an MCP server over stdio (default transport). Expose two tools: (1) analyze_traces(paths: list[str], analyzers=['workflows','patterns','crystallize'], output='json', condense='auto') — runs full pipeline on given paths and returns result as string. (2) get_crystallization_candidates(paths: list[str], min_score=0.6) — runs patterns+crystallize internally and returns only candidates scoring >= min_score as a JSON array. Server is stateless — each tool call is self-contained. Use the mcp Python package.
title: mcp-server: analyze_traces and get_crystallization_candidates tools (stdio)

description: Ensure all internal functions use pathlib.Path instead of mixing with strings.
title: Normalize internal path handling to Path

description: Add -v and -vv flags to khipu analyze. -v logs pipeline decisions to stderr (which ingestor was selected, condensation mode chosen, which analyzers ran). -vv also logs raw LLM prompts and responses for each analyzer. Also add the required run summary line printed after analysis: 'Analyzed N/M files (X skipped: Y parse errors, Z no matching ingestor)'.
title: cli: verbosity flags and run summary line

close_reason: analyze.py: prompt loader, DAG topo sort, JSON extractor with retry, CLI backend executor. AnalysisResult + ResultMetadata dataclasses. Built-in prompts (workflows/patterns/crystallize) and claude_cli.toml backend. 26 tests, 86% total coverage.
description:
Implement the analysis engine: load prompt files with YAML frontmatter, resolve depends_on DAG, inject {sessions}/{patterns}/{workflows} template variables, call LLM backend, extract JSON with retry logic.

Define AnalysisResult and ResultMetadata dataclasses (output types of this stage):
- AnalysisResult: timestamp, session_count, sessions_skipped, workflows, patterns, crystallization, custom, metadata
- ResultMetadata: backend, model, condensation_mode, prompt_versions (dict analyzer_id->version), duration_ms

CLI backend mode: shell out via TOML config. Built-in backend: claude_cli.toml. Command format: pipe prompt via stdin ('echo "$prompt" | claude -p') rather than shell argument to avoid quoting/length issues. context_limit=180000.

Built-in prompts: workflows.md, patterns.md, crystallize.md (all three from the spec). JSON extraction with one retry on parse failure.
title: Analysis engine: DAG runner, prompt loader, Claude CLI backend

description:
Implement the ingestor discovery engine with two-phase detection (extension → content sniff) and priority resolution. Built-in ingestors: claude_code.py (JSONL with tool_use/tool_result), generic.py (markdown fallback, PRIORITY=-100). Engine handles directory recursion; ingestors parse single files.

Stdin contract: when path is '-', extension-based detection is impossible. The engine must require --ingestor flag for stdin input and error clearly if absent: 'Stdin input requires --ingestor flag. Available ingestors: claude_code, generic.' This is the ingestor engine's responsibility, not the CLI wiring layer.
title: Ingestor engine + claude_code and generic built-in ingestors

description: Implement the MCP server command as referenced in justfile and pyproject.toml.
title: Add MCP server implementation

description: Implement automatic redaction that runs before any content reaches an LLM. Default patterns: API keys (sk-, ghp_, Bearer, AKIA), connection strings (postgres://, mongodb://, redis://), private keys, env var assignments, AWS secrets, email addresses, IP addresses. Replace with [REDACTED:type] tokens. --no-redact flag with stderr warning.
title: Redaction: strip secrets before LLM sees anything

description: Add smart condensation mode triggered by --smart-condense CLI flag. Makes one extra LLM call per session batch using an internal _condense.md prompt to produce semantic summaries (paraphrased intent and action descriptions) rather than structural truncation. The _condense.md prompt is prefixed with _ to distinguish it from user-facing analyzers — it must NOT appear in khipu prompts output and must NOT be overridable by user files. The engine must skip any user-provided prompt file whose filename starts with _.
title: condense: smart mode via _condense.md LLM prompt

description: The emit stage currently uses a hardcoded PackageLoader pointing at 'templates/' with .md files. Per spec, emitters should be .j2 files discovered from: built-in 'emitters/' package dir, ~/.config/khipu/emitters/ (user-global), .khipu/emitters/ (project-local, highest priority). User emitters with the same name as a built-in should override it. The json emitter should be a real .j2 template ({{ result | tojson(indent=2) }}) not hardcoded Python. Template context must include: timestamp, session_count, sessions_skipped, workflows, patterns, crystallization, result (full dict), custom analyzer outputs keyed by id.
title: emit: migrate to .j2 discovery with user overrides

description: Naive datetimes in Session.from_dict should be normalized to UTC to avoid TypeError when comparing with aware ones.
title: Session.from_dict does not normalize naive datetime to UTC

description: Add missing analyze command flags per spec: --condense (always deterministic condense), --smart-condense (LLM-based semantic condense, depends on condense smart mode feature), --no-condense (raw sessions, error if too large), --max-sessions N (cap session count), --safe (built-in ingestors only, maps to ingest(safe=True)). These map to existing condense_sessions() mode parameter.
title: cli: condense flags, --max-sessions, and --safe

close_reason: src/khipu package created with __init__.py and cli.py (typer). khipu --version and khipu analyze <path> wired. Tests pass at 100% coverage.
description: Set up pyproject.toml with uv/hatch, khipu package structure, and typer CLI entry point. khipu analyze <path> wired to a no-op stub. khipu --version works. This is the foundation everything else builds on.
title: Project scaffold: khipu package and CLI entry point

close_reason: CLI wired end-to-end: ingest → analyze → emit; --ingestor, --emit, --no-redact, --backend, --model flags; progress to stderr; 9 new CLI tests
description: Connect ingest → redact → condense → analyze → emit into the khipu analyze command. Progress output to stderr, result to stdout. Stdin support: khipu analyze - --ingestor claude_code. Acceptance: khipu analyze ./traces/ produces a markdown report with workflows, patterns, and crystallization scores from real Claude Code session files.
title: Wire end-to-end: khipu analyze runs all 5 stages

description: Two gaps from the CLI spec: (1) analyze command currently accepts a single path argument but spec requires one or more paths (khipu analyze ./a.jsonl ./b.md ./c/). All paths are ingested together as a single session set. (2) --only <analyzers> flag: comma-separated analyzer list as a single string, e.g. --only workflows or --only patterns,crystallize. Maps to the analyzers= parameter of analyze().
title: cli: multi-path support and --only flag

description: The redact spec lists mysql:// as a connection string that should be redacted, but the current _PATTERNS list in redact.py only covers postgres://, mongodb://, and redis://. Add mysql:// to the connection string patterns. Small one-line fix plus a test.
title: redact: add mysql:// pattern
